title.S: Iranian Missile Strike — Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar

description.S: An Iranian missile struck Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar but did not cause any casualties. Qatar's air defenses had been on heightened alert following the previous day's Su-24 intercepts. Canadian military personnel present at the base were confirmed safe by Canadian Defence Minister David McGuinty.

title.S: Iranian Missile Strikes — Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

description.S: Riyadh was struck by direct Iranian missile attacks as part of a sustained campaign against Gulf states. The strikes targeted the Saudi capital and the Eastern Province as Iran sought to pressure Gulf states hosting US military forces.

title.S: Iranian Drone — Crowne Plaza Hotel, Manama

description.S: An Iranian drone struck the Crowne Plaza Hotel in Manama, Bahrain, causing a fire. The attack was part of Iran's sustained campaign against civilian and military infrastructure across Gulf states.

title.S: Iranian Drone Intercepted — Etihad Towers, Abu Dhabi

description.S: An Iranian drone was intercepted near the Etihad Towers complex near the Israeli embassy in Abu Dhabi. Debris from the interception damaged the towers and caused minor injuries to a woman and her child.

title.S: Iranian Drone Strike — Al-Salam Naval Base, Abu Dhabi

description.S: Two Iranian drones targeted a warehouse at Al-Salam Naval Base in Abu Dhabi, causing a fire in two containers storing general materials. The base is operated jointly by the UAE Air Force and the Royal Air Force and houses Australian Defence Force headquarters.

title.S: Iranian Missile — Synagogue Strike, Beit Shemesh

description.S: An Iranian missile breached Israeli defenses and struck a synagogue in Beit Shemesh, killing nine people, leaving 11 missing, and injuring 51 others. The strike was one of the most significant direct hits on Israeli civilian infrastructure during the conflict.

title.S: Iranian Missile — French Base Camp de la Paix, Abu Dhabi

description.S: The French naval base Camp de la Paix in Abu Dhabi was struck by an Iranian missile or drone. France subsequently deployed Rafale jets to protect its bases following the attack. France strongly condemned the strike.

Two one-way attack drones struck the Azerbaijani exclave of Nakhchivan. Azerbaijan blamed Iran and vowed retaliation. Iran denied responsibility, with Foreign Minister Araghchi suggesting it was an Israeli false flag operation. Azerbaijan recalled its diplomatic staff from Iran on March 6.

title.S: Iran Launches 500th Ballistic Missile — Sustained Campaign

description.S: Iranian military sources reported that Iran had fired over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and nearly 2,000 drones since 28 February. Approximately 40% were aimed at Israel and 60% at US targets in the region. Analysts noted declining launch rates pointing to depletion of Iranian missile stocks.

title.S: Israel Strikes 400+ Iranian Targets — Sustained Campaign

description.S: Israeli forces struck over 400 Iranian targets in an effort to cripple Iran's military power, including missile bases and naval ports confirmed by satellite photography. The sustained campaign entered its seventh day with no signs of de-escalation from either side.

title.S: Over 6,668 Civilian Sites Targeted — Iranian Red Crescent Report

description.S: The Iranian Red Crescent Society reported that over 6,668 civilian units had been targeted by US-Israeli strikes since 28 February, including residential buildings, schools, and hospitals. The figure could not be independently verified due to restricted media access inside Iran.

title.S: Iran Fires New Missile Wave at Israel and Gulf States

description.S: Iran launched a new wave of ballistic missiles targeting Israel and US bases across the Gulf on day seven of the conflict. The launches came as Iran's Foreign Ministry stated that military units had been operating according to old general instructions following command disruption.

Clashes between Hezbollah and Israel escalated significantly amid the widening fallout from the war on Iran. Israel pounded Beirut and targets in Lebanon as Hezbollah increased rocket and missile fire into northern Israel, opening a second active front in the conflict.
